title: Kinver: Iron Age Fort & Rock Houses (day outing)

main_route.excerpt: We parked at Keniver for lunch. I'd forgotten the sausages & pork pies so we opened tins of herring (from Germany) & corned beef.

main_route.normalized_name: Kinver Edge Iron Age Hill Fort, Staffordshire, UK
main_route.excerpt: We walked around a marvellous Iron Age Fort, great banks, good views, red earth & not muddy. Lovely open spaces.
There was a sharp drop to the Keniver Rock Houses, so we drove round. In 17 people lived in them until 1969. 2 coal fires, warm & snug.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page B_0091_R contains content from a preceding trip (Bowes/Kendall/M6/Manchester return journey) before the Kinver entry begins
mixed_end: page boundary shared with adjacent trip content
summary: A Saturday day outing on 29 October 2005, in which Simon, Eve and Charles took guests Margaret and Helen to Kinver Edge, Staffordshire. They lunched in the van, walked the Iron Age hill fort, and visited the sandstone Rock Houses, where 17 people had lived until 1969, before returning home to Birmingham the same day.
extraction_notes: The diary page B_0091_R is a mixed-boundary page. The first paragraph (Bowes, Kendall, M6, Manchester) belongs to the preceding trip and has been excluded from this route. The Kinver entry begins at 'October 29th, Saturday 2005.' The dog is referred to as 'Tonic' in the diary, likely a transcription variant of 'Tinder'. Guests Margaret and Helen are not family members and are not included in the travellers list. The phrase 'In 17 people lived in them until 1969' appears to have a transcription gap (likely 'In [all] 17 people...' or 'In [the] [19]17[th century] people...'). No ferries or crossings involved. This is a purely domestic day trip within the West Midlands/Staffordshire area.
